2604.11290.title: Polyglot Teachers: Evaluating Language Models for Multilingual Synthetic Data Generation
2604.11290.paper_summary: Synthesizing supervised finetuning (SFT) data from language models (LMs) to teach smaller models multilingual tasks has become increasingly common. However, teacher model selection is often ad hoc, typically defaulting to the largest available option, even though such models may have significant capability gaps in non-English languages. This practice can result in poor-quality synthetic data and suboptimal student downstream performance. In this work, we systematically characterize what makes an effective multilingual teacher. We measure intrinsic measures of data quality with extrinsic student model performance in a metric we call Polyglot Score; evaluating 10 LMs across 6 typologically diverse languages, generating over 1.4M SFT examples and training 240 student models. Among the models tested, Gemma 3 27B and Aya Expanse 32B emerge as consistently effective teachers across different student base model families. Further analyses reveal that model scale alone does not significantly predict teacher effectiveness; instead, data qualities such as prompt diversity, length, and response fluency capture over 93.3% of variance in intrinsic data quality and predict student performance. Finally, we provide practical recommendations, including matching the model families of teacher-student pairs and translating from or responding to existing prompts, which can yield improvements for less-resourced languages. We hope that our work advances data-centric research in multilingual synthetic data and LM development.
2604.11290.paper_summary_zh: 從語言模型 (LMs) 合成監督微調 (SFT) 數據以教導較小模型多語言任務已變得越來越普遍。<lb/>然而，教師模型的選擇往往是臨時的，通常默認為可用的最大選項，即使這些模型在非英語語言中可能存在顯著的能力差距。<lb/>這種做法可能導致低質量的合成數據和次優的學生下游表現。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們系統地描述了什麼使得一個有效的多語言教師。<lb/>我們測量了數據質量的內在指標與外在學生模型性能之間的關係，並提出了一個我們稱之為 Polyglot Score 的指標；評估了 10 個 LMs 在 6 種類型學上多樣的語言中的表現，生成了超過 140 萬個 SFT 示例並訓練了 240 個學生模型。<lb/>在測試的模型中，Gemma 3 27B 和 Aya Expanse 32B 在不同的學生基礎模型系列中始終表現出色。<lb/>進一步的分析顯示，僅僅依賴模型規模並不能顯著預測教師的有效性；相反，數據質量的特徵，如提示多樣性、長度和回應流暢性，捕捉了超過 93.3% 的內在數據質量的變異並預測學生表現。<lb/>最後，我們提供了實用的建議，包括匹配教師-學生對的模型系列以及從現有提示中翻譯或回應，這可以為資源較少的語言帶來改善。<lb/>我們希望我們的工作能促進以數據為中心的多語言合成數據和 LM 開發的研究。
2604.11290.author: Lester James V. Miranda et.al.
2604.11290.authors: Lester James V. Miranda, Ivan Vulić, Anna Korhonen
